generatedSentence: A blocky jet, moving with thick, strange rhythms, delivered a suspicious plastic package to the hilly city's walkup district. An eyewitness felt a knuckle of fear in their chest as they sensed palpable malice, a lowkey threat lurking in the background of the dimensions they inhabited, suggesting a weird utility to this frumpy arrival, expanding the known geography of crime. The detective's grim rationale led him to investigate the package, finding it full of origami and a grandkid's teddy bear, a bizarre promo for an insane cult marked by a demon's genome. His grandiose plan to unhitch the truth was met with a carjack attempt; a slacker, wearing a mask and a rasta undergarment, launched a volleyball into the detective's face in blatant mockery, whilst rats scurried from canopies. The argument turned into an ambush as the cult leader, resembling a specky giraffe with a vulture's grimace and a flak jacket, galloped forward, shouting judgments. A jackhammer echoed the pounding rhythms of the conflict, as the detective, feeling elemental power, made a swift knock, avoiding the stingray-like wyverns that accompany the gang's violence. The leader's chest was full of rancid entrails, a truly grotesque victim. After the fight, the detective, with a wholly defeated but undefeated spirit, performed a quick checkup, knowing he'd never truly heal the fragility of the city's underbelly. He opened his notebook, a vessel for knowledge, to translate the peculiar facts, knowing his fidelity to justice would always enrich the frontier. He gently counted his ginger-colored pencil, then, for lunch, watched a dolphin play amongst cubic rainbows, feeling a strange volt of peace, as an alabaster spider spun its web.
panelSentences: A blocky jet, moving with thick, strange rhythms, delivered a suspicious plastic package to the hilly city's walkup district. An eyewitness felt a knuckle of fear in their chest as they sensed palpable malice, a lowkey threat lurking in the background of the dimensions they inhabited, suggesting a weird utility to this frumpy arrival, expanding the known geography of crime.

The detective's grim rationale led him to investigate the package, finding it full of origami and a grandkid's teddy bear, a bizarre promo for an insane cult marked by a demon's genome. His grandiose plan to unhitch the truth was met with a carjack attempt; a slacker, wearing a mask and a rasta undergarment, launched a volleyball into the detective's face in blatant mockery, whilst rats scurried from canopies.

The argument turned into an ambush as the cult leader, resembling a specky giraffe with a vulture's grimace and a flak jacket, galloped forward, shouting judgments. A jackhammer echoed the pounding rhythms of the conflict, as the detective, feeling elemental power, made a swift knock, avoiding the stingray-like wyverns that accompany the gang's violence. The leader's chest was full of rancid entrails, a truly grotesque victim.

After the fight, the detective, with a wholly defeated but undefeated spirit, performed a quick checkup, knowing he'd never truly heal the fragility of the city's underbelly. He opened his notebook, a vessel for knowledge, to translate the peculiar facts, knowing his fidelity to justice would always enrich the frontier. He gently counted his ginger-colored pencil, then, for lunch, watched a dolphin play amongst cubic rainbows, feeling a strange volt of peace, as an alabaster spider spun its web.

An eyewitness felt a knuckle of fear in their chest as they sensed palpable malice, a lowkey threat lurking in the background of the dimensions they inhabited, suggesting a weird utility to this frumpy arrival, expanding the known geography of crime.

The detective's grim rationale led him to investigate the package, finding it full of origami and a grandkid's teddy bear, a bizarre promo for an insane cult marked by a demon's genome. His grandiose plan to unhitch the truth was met with a carjack attempt; a slacker, wearing a mask and a rasta undergarment, launched a volleyball into the detective's face in blatant mockery, whilst rats scurried from canopies.

The argument turned into an ambush as the cult leader, resembling a specky giraffe with a vulture's grimace and a flak jacket, galloped forward, shouting judgments. imagePrompt: A professional 4-panel comic book page, 2x2 grid, sequential art, vibrant neon futurism, bold ink outlines, cinematic lighting, thick white gutters, clean black borders. Protagonist: A grizzled detective with a cybernetic eye and a trench coat. TOP-LEFT PANEL: Protagonist examines a glowing, blocky package delivered by a futuristic jet in a rain-slicked neon alley, low angle. TOP-RIGHT PANEL: A masked figure in a vibrant rasta undergarment throws a glowing volleyball towards the protagonist, who is mid-carjack, eye-level shot. BOTTOM-LEFT PANEL: The protagonist, mid-action, punches a grotesque, flak-jacketed cult leader with glowing cybernetic limbs, close-up, dynamic angle. BOTTOM-RIGHT PANEL: The protagonist sits on a rooftop, notebook open, watching a glowing dolphin leap through cubic rainbows in the futuristic city skyline, wide shot.

definitions.definition: The thickest, or most active or intense, part of something.
definitions.example: It was mayhem in the thick of battle.

definitions.definition: A thicket.
A stupid person; a fool.
To thicken.
Relatively great in extent from one surface to the opposite in its smallest solid dimension.
Measuring a certain number of units in this dimension.
definitions.example: I want some planks that are two inches thick.

definitions.definition: Heavy in build; thickset.
definitions.example: He had such a thick neck that he had to turn his body to look to the side.

definitions.definition: Densely crowded or packed.
definitions.example: We walked through thick undergrowth.

definitions.definition: Having a viscous consistency.
definitions.example: My mum’s gravy was thick but at least it moved about.

definitions.definition: Abounding in number.
definitions.example: The room was thick with reporters.

definitions.definition: Impenetrable to sight.
definitions.example: We drove through thick fog.

definitions.definition: (Of an accent) Prominent, strong.
Stupid.
definitions.example: He was as thick as two short planks.

definitions.definition: Friendly or intimate.
definitions.example: They were as thick as thieves.

definitions.definition: Deep, intense, or profound.
definitions.example: Thick darkness.

definitions.definition: Troublesome; unreasonable
(chiefly of women) Curvy and voluptuous, and especially having large hips.
In a thick manner.
definitions.example: Snow lay thick on the ground.

definitions.definition: Frequently or numerously.
definitions.example: The arrows flew thick and fast around us.

definitions.definition: Any of several carrion-eating birds of the families Accipitridae and Cathartidae.
A person who profits from the suffering of others.
definitions.example: Within ten minutes of the accident, the vultures appeared and were organizing lawsuits.

definitions.definition: To circle around one's target as if one were a vulture.
definitions.example: Rudy vultured when asking the girl out.

definitions.definition: A ruminant, of the genus Giraffa, of the African savannah with long legs and highly elongated neck, which make it the tallest living animal; yellow fur patterned with dark spots, often in the form of a network; and two or more short, skin-covered horns, so-called; strictly speaking the horn-like projections are ossicones.
A giraffe unicycle.
A laugh.
definitions.example: Are you having a giraffe?!

definitions.definition: An explanation of the basis or fundamental reasons for something.
A justification or rationalization for something.
The state or condition of being useful; usefulness.
Something that is useful.
The ability of a commodity to satisfy needs or wants; the satisfaction experienced by the consumer of that commodity.
Well-being, satisfaction, pleasure, or happiness.
A service provider, such as an electric company or water company; or, the securities of such a provider.
A software program designed to perform a single task or a small range of tasks, often to help manage and tune computer hardware, an operating system or application software.
definitions.example: I've bought a new disk utility that can recover deleted files.

definitions.definition: The ability to play multiple positions.
Having to do with, or owned by, a service provider.

definitions.definition: Designating of a room in a house or building where mechanical equipment is installed; such as a furnace, water tank/heater, circuit breaker, and/or air conditioning unit; and often equipped with hookups for laundry equipment (washer/dryer).

definitions.definition: To steal an automobile forcibly from (someone).
definitions.example: Someone should carjack that pompous jerk and teach him a lesson!

definitions.definition: To forcibly steal (a vehicle).
definitions.example: These twerps have carjacked their last Mercedes.

definitions.definition: The act of judging.
The power or faculty of performing such operations; especially, when unqualified, the faculty of judging or deciding rightly, justly, or wisely

definitions.definition: The conclusion or result of judging; an opinion; a decision.
The act of determining, as in courts of law, what is conformable to law and justice; also, the determination, decision, or sentence of a court, or of a judge.
The final award; the last sentence.
A collimated stream, spurt or flow of liquid or gas from a pressurized container, an engine, etc.
A spout or nozzle for creating a jet of fluid.
A type of airplane using jet engines rather than propellers.
An engine that propels a vehicle using a stream of fluid as propulsion.
A part of a carburetor that controls the amount of fuel mixed with the air.
A narrow cone of hadrons and other particles produced by the hadronization of a quark or gluon.
Drift; scope; range, as of an argument.
The sprue of a type, which is broken from it when the type is cold.
To spray out of a container.
To spray with liquid from a container.

definitions.definition: To travel on a jet aircraft or otherwise by jet propulsion
To move (running, walking etc.) rapidly around
To shoot forward or out; to project; to jut out.
To strut; to walk with a lofty or haughty gait; to be insolent; to obtrude.
To jerk; to jolt; to be shaken.
To adjust the fuel to air ratio of a carburetor; to install or adjust a carburetor jet
To leave.
Propelled by turbine engines.

definitions.definition: Resembling a block in shape.
definitions.example: The oldest video games had crude, blocky graphics.

definitions.definition: Someone who sees an event and can report or testify about it.
To be present at an event, and see it
A synthetic, solid, hydrocarbon-based polymer, whether thermoplastic or thermosetting.
(metonym) Credit or debit cards used in place of cash to buy goods and services.
Fakeness, or a person who is fake or arrogant, or believes that they are better than the rest of the population.
An instance of plastic surgery.

Creative, formative.

Inferior or not the real thing.
(of a person) Fake.

A package holiday.

A group of related stories spread over several pages.

To yield.

definitions.definition: A flow, repetition or regularity.

definitions.definition: The tempo or speed of a beat, song or repetitive event.

definitions.definition: The musical instruments which provide rhythm (mainly; not or less melody) in a musical ensemble.

definitions.definition: A regular quantitative change in a variable (notably natural) process.

definitions.definition: Controlled repetition of a phrase, incident or other element as a stylistic figure in literature and other narrative arts; the effect it creates.
definitions.example: The running gag is a popular rhythm in motion pictures and theater comedy

definitions.definition: An artificial method of preventing sensation, used to eliminate pain without causing loss of vital functions, by the administration of one or more agents which block pain impulses before transmitted to the brain.

A fact or statement used to support a proposition; a reason.

A value, or reference to a value, passed to a function.

definitions.definition: A parameter at a function call; an actual parameter, as opposed to a formal parameter.
Any of the phrases that bears a syntactic connection to the verb of a clause.

To change from a smaller form/size to a larger one.

A special piece of clothing worn by soldiers to protect them from shrapnel and grenade fragments, other non-direct fire and battlefield debris. Sometimes used informally to connote any of the various forms of a bullet-proof vest.

A single aspect of a given thing.

Rank in taste or smell.

Having a visible or identifying mark.
Clearly evident; noticeable; conspicuous.

definitions.definition: Something which is real.

definitions.definition: Something concrete used as a basis for further interpretation.

definitions.definition: An objective consensus on a fundamental reality that has been agreed upon by a substantial number of experts.

definitions.definition: Information about a particular subject, especially actual conditions and/or circumstances.

Action; the realm of action.

definitions.definition: A feat or meritorious deed.
Faithfulness to one's duties.

definitions.definition: Loyalty to one's spouse or partner, including abstention from extramarital affairs.

Mimicry, imitation, now usually in a derogatory sense; a travesty, a ridiculous simulacrum.

definitions.definition: Applause; applauding.
Plaudit.

definitions.definition: To disconnect; to detach; to undo that which is hitched.
The act of counting or tallying a quantity.

definitions.definition: The result of a tally that reveals the number of items in a set; a quantity counted.
A countdown.
A charge of misconduct brought in a legal proceeding.
The number of balls and strikes, respectively, on a batter's in-progress plate appearance.
definitions.example: He has a 3–2 count with the bases loaded.

definitions.definition: An object of interest or account; value; estimation.
To recite numbers in sequence.
definitions.example: Can you count to a hundred?

definitions.definition: To determine the number (of objects in a group).
definitions.example: There are three apples; count them.

definitions.definition: To be of significance; to matter.
definitions.example: Your views don’t count here.    It does count if you cheat with someone when you’re drunk.

definitions.definition: To be an example of something: often followed by as and an indefinite noun.
definitions.example: Apples count as a type of fruit.

definitions.definition: To consider something an example of something.
definitions.example: He counts himself a hero after saving the cat from the river.   I count you as more than a friend.

definitions.definition: To take account or note (of).
To plead orally; to argue a matter in court; to recite a count.
(grammar) Countable.
(of a landscape) Abundant in hills; having many hills.
Exceptional in importance or significance; special.
